title: Java Method Overloading
content:
### Method Overloading

Method overloading means having multiple methods with the same name but different parameters in the same class.

---

### Rules for Method Overloading

Methods must differ in at least one of:
- Number of parameters
- Data type of parameters
- Order of parameters

Return type alone is NOT sufficient for overloading.

---

### Examples of Overloads

| Method Signature | Description |
|-----------------|-------------|
| `add(int, int)` | Two integers |
| `add(int, int, int)` | Three integers (different count) |
| `add(double, double)` | Two doubles (different type) |
| `add(int, double)` | Different types (different order) |

---

### Type Promotion in Overloading

```java
void display(int a) { ... }
void display(double a) { ... }

display(5);     // calls display(int)
display(5.5);   // calls display(double)
display('A');   // char is promoted to int
```

---

### Overloading and Varargs

```java
void show(String s) { ... }
void show(String... s) { ... }

show("hi");     // calls String version (preferred)
show("a", "b"); // calls varargs version
```

> **Key Point:** Overloading improves code readability by using the same method name for similar operations. The compiler determines which overload to call at compile time (compile-time polymorphism).

quiz.question: Can method overloading be done by changing only the return type?
When is overloaded method resolution determined?
